title: phaseolus vulgaris var. luteomaculatus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. luteomaculatus, also known as the yellow-spotted kidney bean, is a variant of the common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) characterized by its distinctive yellow spots. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The yellow-spotted kidney bean is also believed to have several health benefits, including reducing blood sugar levels, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive function. In herbal medicine, the beans are often prepared as a decoction or tea, or dried and consumed as a capsule or supplement, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
